content.text: Aggregating multiple Git repositories, wikis, and document stores into a single

lets Claude answer questions that span organizational silos. The key challenge is that each repo has its own structure, vocabulary, access rules, and update cadence — your ingestion pipeline must normalise all of that into a consistent metadata schema so downstream retrieval can filter, rank, and attribute correctly.

attrs.alt: Multi-repo aggregation pipeline: source repos → normalizer → chunk+embed → unified vector store

content.text: Every chunk stored in the unified index must carry a fixed set of provenance fields. Without them, results are unattributable and access filtering is impossible.

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional

@dataclass
class ChunkMetadata:
    # Provenance
    repo: str               # "engineering-docs", "product-wiki"
    doc_path: str           # "architecture/overview.md"
    doc_id: str             # stable ID: sha256(repo + doc_path)
    chunk_index: int        # position within document

    # Content classification
    doc_type: str           # "markdown", "confluence", "notion", "pdf"
    language: str           # "en", "fr"
    section: Optional[str]  # heading breadcrumb if extractable

    # Access control
    access_level: str       # "public", "internal", "restricted"
    team_labels: list[str]  # ["engineering", "security"]

    # Temporal
    source_updated_at: datetime
    indexed_at: datetime

    # Embedding provenance
    embedding_model: str    # "cohere/embed-english-v3.0"
    embedding_version: str  # "v2"
is the most important field. Compute it as
so it is stable across re-index runs and lets you upsert (overwrite) a chunk without duplicating it.
Each source type needs an adapter that converts its native format into the canonical schema. Write one adapter class per source type — not one per repo.

import hashlib
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime, timezone

class GitMarkdownAdapter:
    """Ingest Markdown files from a cloned Git repository."""

    def __init__(self, repo_name: str, repo_path: Path,
                 access_level: str = "internal",
                 team_labels: list[str] | None = None):
        self.repo_name = repo_name
        self.repo_path = repo_path
        self.access_level = access_level
        self.team_labels = team_labels or []

    def iter_documents(self):
        for md_file in self.repo_path.rglob("*.md"):
            rel = md_file.relative_to(self.repo_path).as_posix()
            text = md_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
            doc_id = hashlib.sha256(
                f"{self.repo_name}:{rel}".encode()
            ).hexdigest()[:16]
            stat_mtime = md_file.stat().st_mtime
            updated = datetime.fromtimestamp(stat_mtime, tz=timezone.utc)
            yield {
                "repo":           self.repo_name,
                "doc_path":       rel,
                "doc_id":         doc_id,
                "text":           text,
                "doc_type":       "markdown",
                "access_level":   self.access_level,
                "team_labels":    self.team_labels,
                "source_updated_at": updated,
            }
The pipeline pulls from all adapters, chunks each document, embeds the chunks in batches, and upserts them into the vector store with their full metadata.
When Claude issues a retrieval query, the filter must constrain results to chunks the
requesting user is allowed to read
. Never rely solely on post-retrieval filtering — always push the access constraint into the vector store query so

from qdrant_client.models import Filter, FieldCondition, MatchAny

def retrieve_for_user(
    query_embedding: list[float],
    user_access_levels: list[str],  # ["public", "internal"]
    user_teams: list[str],          # ["engineering", "product"]
    repo_allowlist: list[str] | None = None,  # None = all repos
    top_k: int = 8,
) -> list[dict]:
    must = [
        FieldCondition(
            key="access_level",
            match=MatchAny(any=user_access_levels)
        )
    ]
    if repo_allowlist:
        must.append(FieldCondition(
            key="repo",
            match=MatchAny(any=repo_allowlist)
        ))

    results = qdrant.search(
        collection_name=COLLECTION,
        query_vector=query_embedding,
        query_filter=Filter(must=must),
        limit=top_k,
        with_payload=True,
    )
    return [
        {
            "score":    hit.score,
            "text":     hit.payload["text"],
            "repo":     hit.payload["repo"],
            "doc_path": hit.payload["doc_path"],
            "updated":  hit.payload["source_updated_at"],
        }
        for hit in results
    ]
Group retrieved chunks by repo and cite each one with its source path. This gives Claude enough attribution information to qualify its answers and lets users verify claims.
When the same topic exists in multiple repos (e.g., both engineering-docs and product-wiki explain authentication),
at index time — preserve all versions with their
metadata. Let Claude synthesise conflicting information explicitly by sourcing each claim.

-- Find topics that appear in more than one repo (similarity > 0.92)
-- Useful for audit, not for automatic dedup
SELECT
    a.payload->'doc_path' AS path_a,
    b.payload->'doc_path' AS path_b,
    a.payload->'repo'     AS repo_a,
    b.payload->'repo'     AS repo_b,
    1 - (a.embedding <=> b.embedding) AS cosine_similarity
FROM chunks a
JOIN chunks b ON a.id < b.id
WHERE a.payload->>'repo' != b.payload->>'repo'
  AND 1 - (a.embedding <=> b.embedding) > 0.92
ORDER BY cosine_similarity DESC
LIMIT 50;

Run this query weekly and route high-similarity cross-repo pairs to a human reviewer. Flag discrepancies in the Claude context with a note like: "Two sources cover this topic — verify with the canonical owner."
in vector store, hard-delete after 24 h
Run full ingest for new repo; existing chunks unaffected
Trigger targeted re-upsert for affected doc_ids (not full rebuild)
Keep both; differ by repo field; surface both to Claude
filter to retrieval when query language is detected
generation: if the same file gets a different ID on each run, you accumulate duplicate embeddings. Hash the stable (repo, path) pair, not content.
Over-broad retrieval scope: returning chunks from every repo when only one is relevant dilutes precision. Let the query include an optional
Missing access filter in search path: a code path that bypasses
filtering exposes restricted content to unpermissioned users.
Single embedding model across languages: multilingual documents need a multilingual embedding model (e.g.,
) — English-only models produce garbage vectors for other languages.
Ingesting binary files (PDFs, DOCX) without text extraction: store only the extracted plaintext, never the binary.

Multi-repo aggregation works when every chunk carries stable provenance metadata — repo, doc_path, doc_id, access_level, and embedding_version. Write one adapter per source type, not per repo. Push access constraints into the vector store filter rather than post-filtering. Preserve cross-repo duplicates and let Claude surface both sources, flagging discrepancies. Incremental updates via doc_id-based upserts keep the index fresh without full rebuilds.
